zh.lifeSummary: Simon De MONTFORT 1V出生在英國。他於1240年之前與wife Simon De Montfort IV在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Simon De MONTFORT 1V出生在英国。他于1240年之前与wife Simon De Montfort IV在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: MONTFORT
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and Breton: variant of   Monfort   1. This surname is most common in Brittany and Haute-Savoie. See also 2 below.
Altered form of Dutch Montfoort: habitational name from Montfoort in Utrecht. It is a cognate of 1 above (see Monfort 1) and also its altered form (or perhaps vice versa).
English (of Norman origin): very rare variant of   Mountford  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Monfort
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Jacques, Marcel, Monique, Wilner.

French and Breton: habitational name from any of numerous places called Montfort, from Old French mont ‘hill’ (see Mont 1) + fort ‘strong, impregnable’ (from Latin fortis). This surname is by far most common in Brittany. It is also found in Haiti. Compare Montfort 1, see also 2 below.
Altered form of Dutch Montfoort, a variant of Montfort 2 and also a cognate of the French name in 1 above and an altered form of its variant Montfort 1 (or perhaps vice versa).

variants.name: Monforte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Carmela, Carmine, Rocco, Rosaria, Sal.  Spanish Carlos, Francisco, Luis, Medardo.

Italian and Spanish: habitational name from any of numerous places called Monforte, from Latin mons ‘mountain’ (genitive montis) + fortis ‘strong’, probably a reference to the strategic position of these locations.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Montfort  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: Simon De MONTFORT 1V was born in England. He married wife Simon De Montfort IV before 1240, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Simon De MONTFORT 1V est né à Angleterre. Il a épousé wife Simon De Montfort IV avant 1240, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Simon De MONTFORT 1V wurde in England geboren. Er hat vor 1240 in Europa wife Simon De Montfort IV geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Simon De MONTFORT 1V è nato il nel England. Lui sposato wife Simon De Montfort IV prima del 1240, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Simon De MONTFORT 1Vはイギリスで生まれた。1240年以前にヨーロッパでwife Simon De Montfort IVと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Simon De MONTFORT 1V(은)는 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 1240년 이전 우럽에서 wife Simon De Montfort IV(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Simon De MONTFORT 1V nasceu em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com wife Simon De Montfort IV antes 1240, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Simon De MONTFORT 1V родился в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени wife Simon De Montfort IV перед 1240 г. в местности Европа. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Simon De MONTFORT 1V nació en Inglaterra. Se casó con wife Simon De Montfort IV antes de 1240, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en su pueblo.
